examples.note: Dynamic segment :id can be any string. Access via useParams().
Optional parameter — matches both /stories and /stories/123, but NOT /stories/123/comments.
Optional parameter matched with a value present.
Bare catch-all * matches any remaining path segments. Used for 404 pages.
Named catch-all: the matched remainder is accessible via useParams().rest.
Nested route: child Route rendered inside parent via props.children (replaces removed <Outlet> in v0.10+).

clientDetection.value: isServer from 'solid-js/web' — pass url prop on server: <Router url={isServer ? req.url : ''} />

hooks.name: useBeforeLeave
hooks.access: Must be called inside a component that is rendered within the router tree (reactive component scope).

availableApi.name: e.from
availableApi.description: Current Location object before the navigation (the page being left).

availableApi.name: e.to
availableApi.description: The path (string | number) passed to navigate — the intended destination.

availableApi.name: e.preventDefault
availableApi.description: Function — call to block the navigation.

availableApi.name: e.defaultPrevented
availableApi.description: Readonly boolean — true if a previous leave handler called preventDefault.

availableApi.name: e.retry
availableApi.description: Function (force?: boolean) — retry the same navigation, optionally skipping leave handlers when force=true.

hooks.name: useIsRouting (createEffect pattern)
hooks.access: Must be called inside a component within the router tree. Returns a reactive signal.

availableApi.name: isRouting()
availableApi.description: Boolean signal — true during any navigation transition after initial render, false once the transition ends (nested Suspense may still be loading).

hooks.name: useLocation (createEffect pattern)
hooks.access: Must be called inside a component within the router tree. Returns a reactive proxy.

availableApi.name: location.pathname
availableApi.description: Reactive — the current URL path (e.g. /users/42). Updates after navigation commits.

availableApi.name: location.state
availableApi.description: Reactive — arbitrary state stored in history.state passed via useNavigate.

hooks.name: useCurrentMatches (createEffect pattern)
hooks.access: Must be called inside a component within the router tree. Returns a reactive signal.

availableApi.name: matches()
availableApi.description: Array of all RouteMatch objects for the currently matched route tree. Each match exposes m.route.path and m.route.info.

availableApi.name: m.route.path
availableApi.description: The route pattern that matched this segment (e.g. /users/:id).

availableApi.name: m.route.info
availableApi.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the route definition via its info property.

routingChanges: Removed <Routes> component — <Router> now acts as <Routes>; children must be <Route> components.
Removed useRoutes — pass config array directly as children of <Router>.
Removed data functions and useRouteData — replaced by load/preload mechanism.
No Context API available in non-hydrating (Islands) parts of the application.
Top-level layout moved to root prop of <Router>.
Renamed load to preload on route definitions.
Response helpers now return responses; cache/action filter them out.
Default form actions changed to URL-encoded format.
Renamed cache to query for data-fetching API.

notes: Solid Router uses SolidJS fine-grained reactivity for all navigation primitives — useLocation, useIsRouting, useCurrentMatches all return reactive signals/proxies that update automatically when navigation commits. The canonical pattern for tracking route changes for analytics is createEffect(() => { ... location.pathname ... }) inside a layout component. useBeforeLeave fires before the navigation completes and does NOT fire reliably on browser back/forward buttons (known limitation). useIsRouting is backed by SolidJS Transitions and becomes true during navigation, false after the transition ends — but nested <Suspense> may still be resolving at that point. The <Outlet> component was removed in v0.10 in favour of passing props.children. File-based routing is available via SolidStart's <FileRoutes> component. Route parameter validation is supported via the matchFilters prop on <Route>. Three router variants exist: <Router> (history/pushState), <HashRouter> (hash-based, client-only), <MemoryRouter> (in-memory, for testing). SSR is handled by passing a url prop to <Router> server-side, detected via isServer from solid-js/web.
